texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince is a computer program that converts XML and HTML into PDF. It is simple, yet very powerful, and it creates beautiful documents. The purpose of this small document is to showcase the formatting magic Prince can do. We have chosen to highlight eighteen of our favorite features. This document is written in HTML and converted to PDF by Prince. The source file is a compact 13k document, including the embedded CSS, SVG and MathML.
texts.text: Prince is a computer program that converts XML and HTML into PDF. It is simple, yet very powerful, and it creates beautiful documents. The purpose of this small document is to showcase the formatting magic Prince can do. We have chosen to highlight eighteen of our favorite features. This document is written in HTML and converted to PDF by Prince. The source file is a compact 13k document, including the embedded CSS, SVG and MathML.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince 6 supports automatic hyphenation which can break words across several lines, adding a hyphen at the word break. Hyphenation is controlled with a set of experimental  CSS  properties,  and  hyphenation  patterns  for  different languages can be supplied. Notice how the text in this document is hyphenated.
texts.text: Prince 6 supports automatic hyphenation which can break words across several lines, adding a hyphen at the word break. Hyphenation is controlled with a set of experimental  CSS  properties,  and  hyphenation  patterns  for  different languages can be supplied. Notice how the text in this document is hyphenated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CSS3 introduces support for rounded borders. In the table below, some of the corners have been rounded. On purpose, the bottom right corner has an asymmetrical shape.
texts.text: CSS3 introduces support for rounded borders. In the table below, some of the corners have been rounded. On purpose, the bottom right corner has an asymmetrical shape.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It's  sometimes convenient to replace one character with another  without  changing  the  source  document.  For  example, the apostrophe character is easily found on keyboards, but in print it's common to replace it with a quotation character. Notice how Prince 6 has replaced the apostrophes in this paragraph.
texts.text: It's  sometimes convenient to replace one character with another  without  changing  the  source  document.  For  example, the apostrophe character is easily found on keyboards, but in print it's common to replace it with a quotation character. Notice how Prince 6 has replaced the apostrophes in this paragraph.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince 6 can fetch fonts from the web and use them without installing them on your system. The fonts used in the PDF version of this document are web fonts. We are grateful  to Ray  Larabie, Dieter  Steffmann,  and Red  Hat for making hi-quality fonts freely available.
texts.text: Prince 6 can fetch fonts from the web and use them without installing them on your system. The fonts used in the PDF version of this document are web fonts. We are grateful  to Ray  Larabie, Dieter  Steffmann,  and Red  Hat for making hi-quality fonts freely available.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sometimes images should be scaled to a certain resolution, rather than to an absolute size. In Prince, you can set the resolution of an image as a property in the style sheet. The smiley face in the previous section was scaled this way.  The  smiley  also  represents  the  challenging Acid2 test, which Prince 6 passes.
texts.text: Sometimes images should be scaled to a certain resolution, rather than to an absolute size. In Prince, you can set the resolution of an image as a property in the style sheet. The smiley face in the previous section was scaled this way.  The  smiley  also  represents  the  challenging Acid2 test, which Prince 6 passes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Columns are commonly used  on  paper  and  Prince supports multi-column layouts described in CSS. This document is laid out in two columns. Also, this section

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Printers don't use RGB colors, they mostly use CMYK: cyan,  magenta,  yellow  and  black.  Prince 6  can  read CMYK colors and will use them, if present. The heading above  this  paragraph  has  both  an  RGB  color  (red)  and a  CMYK  color  (bluish).  Therefore,  the  text  is  red  in browsers, but blue in the PDF version. This is for demon-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince 6 has built-in HTTP support and can fetch pages, images, DTDs and style sheets from the web. The image to the left was automatically fetched when the PDF version was generated.
texts.text: Prince 6 has built-in HTTP support and can fetch pages, images, DTDs and style sheets from the web. The image to the left was automatically fetched when the PDF version was generated.

texts.orig: uses  a  two-column  layout with  gap  and  a  rule  between. The width of the gap and the style of the rule is set in the style sheet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you are reading the XHTML source code of this document, look for the h2 elements. You will notice that they contain  the  text  of  the  headings,  but  not  their  number. The list  item  number,  including  the '#' and ':' are automatically generated by the style sheet. Generated content and counters are especially useful for complex documents. They are described in CSS level 2.1.
texts.text: If you are reading the XHTML source code of this document, look for the h2 elements. You will notice that they contain  the  text  of  the  headings,  but  not  their  number. The list  item  number,  including  the '#' and ':' are automatically generated by the style sheet. Generated content and counters are especially useful for complex documents. They are described in CSS level 2.1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  printing,  crop  marks  are  used  to  indicate  where  the printed paper should be cut. Cross marks are used to align prints  of  different  colors  to  improve  color  reproduction. Prince 6 adds support for crop and cross marks, and the PDF version of this document includes both.
texts.text: In  printing,  crop  marks  are  used  to  indicate  where  the printed paper should be cut. Cross marks are used to align prints  of  different  colors  to  improve  color  reproduction. Prince 6 adds support for crop and cross marks, and the PDF version of this document includes both.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince can read hyperlinks inside a document and generate page numbers accordingly. For example, it will automatically find out which page Headers and Footers are discussed on (page 2). Cross-references are used to generate the Table of contents (page 1).
texts.text: Prince can read hyperlinks inside a document and generate page numbers accordingly. For example, it will automatically find out which page Headers and Footers are discussed on (page 2). Cross-references are used to generate the Table of contents (page 1).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Footnotes 1 are essential in printed documents and Prince knows how to generate them. Unlike what some people think, footnotes are not the place to put information you don't want to see. More often, footnotes will actually attract attention. 9 of 10 of readers will read the footnotes before they read the text from where the footnotes are an-
texts.text: Footnotes 1 are essential in printed documents and Prince knows how to generate them. Unlike what some people think, footnotes are not the place to put information you don't want to see. More often, footnotes will actually attract attention. 9 of 10 of readers will read the footnotes before they read the text from where the footnotes are an-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. A footnote is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a book or manuscript that comments on or cites a reference for a designated part of the text.
texts.text: A footnote is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a book or manuscript that comments on or cites a reference for a designated part of the text.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Often, the most interesting information is found in the footnotes.
texts.text: Often, the most interesting information is found in the footnotes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Leaders consist of dots or dashes in a row leading the eye across a page. For example, the Table of contents has leaders in it. The leaders are not found in the document itself, but rather in the style sheet.
texts.text: Leaders consist of dots or dashes in a row leading the eye across a page. For example, the Table of contents has leaders in it. The leaders are not found in the document itself, but rather in the style sheet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Scalable  Vector  Graphics  (SVG)  is  a  language for describing two-dimensional graphics for the web. SVG images scale better than traditional bitmapped images and are suitable
texts.text: Scalable  Vector  Graphics  (SVG)  is  a  language for describing two-dimensional graphics for the web. SVG images scale better than traditional bitmapped images and are suitable

texts.label: text
texts.orig: for  printing.  The  crown  is  generated  by  two  SVG  elements.
texts.text: for  printing.  The  crown  is  generated  by  two  SVG  elements.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On paged media, elements can be set float to the top or bottom  of  pages.  The  big  URL  at  the  top  of  this  page comes after this paragraph in the source code, but is floated to the top by the style sheet.
texts.text: On paged media, elements can be set float to the top or bottom  of  pages.  The  big  URL  at  the  top  of  this  page comes after this paragraph in the source code, but is floated to the top by the style sheet.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Printed documents often have page headers and footers. For example, page numbers are often printed at the bottom of the page, and the document title is shown at the top - except on title pages.
texts.text: Printed documents often have page headers and footers. For example, page numbers are often printed at the bottom of the page, and the document title is shown at the top - except on title pages.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prince will automatically generate PDF bookmarks from heading  elements  in  HTML.  The  feature  is  set  with  a property in the style sheet, and can also be used with other markup languages.
texts.text: Prince will automatically generate PDF bookmarks from heading  elements  in  HTML.  The  feature  is  set  with  a property in the style sheet, and can also be used with other markup languages.
